data.title: Reduce ticket CLI subprocess overhead via sourceable ticket-lib API
data.description:
## Summary

The ticket CLI dispatcher (`plugins/dso/scripts/ticket`) adds ~0.3-0.5s overhead per invocation due to bash subprocess startup, `ticket-lib.sh` sourcing, path resolution, and Python reducer invocation. Test files that make 20-60+ ticket CLI calls per run spend 10-25s on dispatcher overhead alone.

## Evidence (measured 2026-04-12)

| Operation | Per-call cost | Typical calls/test | Overhead |
|-----------|--------------|-------------------|----------|
| `ticket create` | 0.44s | 20-22 | 9-10s |
| `ticket link/unlink` | 0.30s | 10-25 | 3-8s |
| `ticket transition` | 0.35s | 10-20 | 4-7s |
| `ticket comment` | 0.30s | 5-10 | 2-3s |

18 ticket test files in the slow list (>5s) are bottlenecked by this overhead.

## Proposed Approach

Create a sourceable `ticket-lib-api.sh` (or extend the existing `ticket-lib.sh`) that exposes ticket operations as bash functions callable without subprocess dispatch:

```bash
source "$PLUGIN_ROOT/plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh"
ticket_create task "My ticket"     # returns ID via stdout, no subprocess
ticket_link "$id1" "$id2" blocks   # direct function call
ticket_transition "$id" open closed --reason="Fixed: summary"
```

### Key design constraints
- Must use the same event-sourcing primitives as the CLI dispatcher (no logic duplication)
- Must maintain flock serialization for concurrent safety
- Must support the same validation (closed-parent rejection, cycle detection, etc.)
- The CLI dispatcher should become a thin wrapper around the sourceable API

### Success Criteria
1. SC1: `ticket-lib-api.sh` can be sourced in test files, providing `ticket_create`, `ticket_link`, `ticket_transition`, `ticket_comment` functions
2. SC2: Each function call completes in <0.05s (vs current 0.3-0.5s)
3. SC3: All existing ticket test assertions pass with the sourceable API
4. SC4: The CLI dispatcher (`plugins/dso/scripts/ticket`) delegates to the same functions (no logic duplication)
5. SC5: Concurrent safety (flock) is preserved

### Impact
Estimated 60-70% reduction in ticket test runtime across 18 slow tests. Total suite savings: ~120-180s.
